request.label: memory_attribution
request.transcript: Linell's pictures are a sort of upgard's and adam paintings, and mason's exquisite idylls are as national as a jingo poem. Mister burke foster's landscapes smile at one much in the same way that mister carker used to flash his teeth, and mister john collier gives his sitter a cheerful slap on the back before he says, like a shampooer in a turkish bath, next man.
